summary: Cycle J (10th cron fire of UTC 2026-05-24; cycle I closed 15:01:55Z, cycle J opens 20:00:57Z = ~5h 0min wall-clock gap — SECOND consecutive non-~1h gap, further weakening cycle G/H's '~1h dominant mode' over-claim that cycle I already downgraded). Ran heartbeat-only. Both queues absent on disk for 44th consecutive cycle. regen-all clean: ALL CHECKS PASSED at 20:01:44Z, round-trip 0 failures sustained (24s elapsed — BACK TO 4-cycle plateau baseline D+F+G+H 24s; cycle I's +3s tick confound hypothesis VALIDATED via natural experiment of cycle J = no concurrent Founder activity; heartbeat written 20:02:03Z status=PASS 4-field schema). THREE GENUINE NEW OBSERVATIONS: (1) CADENCE FURTHER WEAKENED — I→J gap = ~5h, SECOND consecutive non-~1h gap. Twelve observations total: M→A ~5h, A→B ~53min, B→C ~60.5min, C→D ~59min, D→E ~59min, E→F ~60min, F→G ~60min, G→H ~60min, H→I ~3h, I→J ~5h. Diagnosis FURTHER DOWNGRADED from cycle I's 'bimodal/multimodal with possible mid-mode at ~3h' to 'irregular post-cycle-A; sample of 9 post-A gaps spans 53min to ~5h; insufficient evidence to claim any specific schedule'. Three reframes from cycle I still applicable but each strained. (2) CYCLE I METRIC-INTEGRITY CORRECTION — cycle I claimed '361dcffe is FIRST non-cron commit since cycle A reset' but commit 958e9ca5 feat(quick-search): Cmd-K / Ctrl-K command palette at 12:31:23Z (~30min after cycle H closed) was ALSO in H→I window. TWO non-cron commits in H→I window, not one. Cycle I missed the earlier one because it landed before cycle I's session opened. Cycle J caught the over-claim by independently checking git log. (3) HEARTBEAT-DURATION CONFOUND VALIDATED via natural experiment — cycle I had concurrent Founder commit (361dcffe) → 27s duration; cycle J has no concurrent activity → 24s baseline. Hypothesis confirmed; pattern now safer: 5-cycle 24s plateau (D+F+G+H+J) with cycle I as documented exception. FOUNDER ABSENCE in I→J window — no non-cron commits between 361dcffe (15:01:44Z) and cycle J probe (20:00:57Z) = ~5h gap; Founder appears idle after W4.I1 dimming work. TOKEN THRESHOLD PERSISTS 8th cross-cycle cycle — cumulative tokens_consumed_since_last_rest = ~345k (was 315k at cycle I; +30k cycle J). thresholds_crossed=['tokens_consumed'] PRESERVED. Founder-decision still LIVE for 9th cycle. Untracked tree unchanged from cycle I (7 items preserved; no new Founder dev artifacts in I→J window corroborating Founder absence). WELLNESS: status=active throughout (no rest triggered). Counters: tokens accumulate continuously (~345k cumulative, threshold-crossed-persisted 8th cross-cycle cycle); hours_active discrete-context (0.5h). Single yellow user-context-gate continues (14034.2 min, ~9.74 days). Metric-integrity attestation: HONEST. Three genuine new observations corroborated by independent evidence (git log spans cycle I to cycle J, heartbeat-file diff, wall-clock + stale-counter dual agreement, post-commit hook log presence). The cycle-I-correction is exactly what cycle I itself warned about ('Resist over-claiming') — cycle J validates that caution by catching cycle I's own over-claim. Eight carry-over Founder action items preserved from cycle I close; #1 (cadence) further downgraded with cycle I claim correction noted; #8 (token-counter) persistence-flag preserved 8th cross-cycle cycle.
founder_action_items: Cron cadence — 28th observation; diagnosis FURTHER DOWNGRADED from cycle I's 'bimodal/multimodal with possible mid-mode at ~3h, 8-observation post-cycle-A sample' to 'irregular post-cycle-A; sample of 9 post-A gaps spans 53min to ~5h; insufficient evidence to claim any specific schedule'. Cycle J's I→J gap = ~5h is the SECOND consecutive non-~1h gap (after H→I ~3h). Twelve observations: M→A ~5h, A→B ~53min, B→C ~60.5min, C→D ~59min, D→E ~59min, E→F ~60min, F→G ~60min, G→H ~60min, H→I ~3h, I→J ~5h. Three reframes from cycle I still applicable but each strained: (a) event-triggered (no Founder activity in I→J argues against), (b) Founder-active windows suppress cron (does not explain I→J), (c) wider multimodal cluster, (d) original ~1h schedule with missed fires (would need 8 missed fires across two consecutive gaps). Founder-action: inspect cron schedule source OR add Step-0 guard. CYCLE I CLAIM CORRECTION: cycle I stated '361dcffe is FIRST non-cron commit since cycle A reset' — INCORRECT; commit 958e9ca5 feat(quick-search): Cmd-K / Ctrl-K command palette at 12:31:23Z was ALSO in H→I window (~30min after cycle H closed). Two non-cron commits in H→I window, not one. Metric-integrity correction logged at cycle J.
A8_performance: Lighthouse 65/100 on 1 page (target 75+) — carry-over from cycles S→I; rolling-window mechanics.

main-flows.html user-context capture ~9.74 days stale at cycle J (was 9.54 at cycle I; +300.2 min consistent with ~5h I→J gap + ~1 min regen-window) — run `node scripts/visual-audit/founder-context-capture.mjs` before next ship-close.
quota-status weekly_cap field still null — no % computation possible (preserved from cycles R→I).

post-commit aggregate-self-tests FAIL on 2 aggregators per cycle F's metric-integrity correction: aggregate-test-health parity-fail (source-detected but status=unknown) + aggregate-security-health stale-timestamp. Founder-action: investigate whether test-health source-detection / status-mapping is broken, and why security-health timestamp is stale. Carry-over wording locked at cycle F.
A12_operational — sustained yellow with continued oscillation across rolling-window mechanics.

Heartbeat `status: GATE-FAIL` field semantics — DUAL-WRITE-PATH MODEL VALIDATED at cycle J via duration confound experiment (cycle I had concurrent commit → 27s duration; cycle J no concurrent commit → 24s baseline). Confirms post-commit hook fires from .husky/post-commit in parallel with cron's regen-all when a non-cron commit lands during a cron cycle. Hook log content (post-commit-hook.log entry for 361dcffe) was modified in cycle I's tree but cycle J did not inspect its content (heartbeat-only triage discipline). Founder-action remains: inspect .husky/post-commit source AND post-commit-hook.log entry for commit 361dcffe to determine whether the hook wrote GATE-FAIL or PASS for this non-routine commit, and reconcile with the 4-field PASS schema written by cron's regen-all path.

Wellness token-counter semantics — THRESHOLD PERSISTS 8th cross-cycle cycle. Cumulative tokens_consumed_since_last_rest = ~345k (was 315k at cycle I; +30k cycle J). thresholds_crossed=['tokens_consumed'] PRESERVED. Projection method continues to track across FOUR checkpoints now (E:102.5% of 200k, H:95% of 300k, I:105% of 300k, J:~115% of 300k). Founder-decision still LIVE for 9th cycle: (a) reset tokens per cron fire, (b) raise threshold, (c) auto-trigger rest when crossed-while-active, (d) leave current convention (current path).
metric_integrity_corrections_this_cycle: Cycle I CORRECTION — cycle I claimed '361dcffe is FIRST non-cron commit since cycle A reset (~10h earlier)' but commit 958e9ca5 feat(quick-search): Cmd-K / Ctrl-K command palette (W2.S5 G2) at 2026-05-24 08:31:23 -0400 = 12:31:23Z was also in H→I window (~30min after cycle H closed at 12:01:19Z). TWO non-cron commits in H→I window, not one. Cycle I noticed only the one that landed concurrently with its own session. Cycle J caught the over-claim by independently checking git log rather than trusting cycle I's narrative. This is metric-integrity protocol functioning as designed.

Cadence diagnosis FURTHER DOWNGRADE — cycle I downgraded from cycles G/H's 'effectively confirmed ~1h dominant mode' to 'bimodal/multimodal with possible mid-mode at ~3h'. Cycle J's I→J ~5h gap (SECOND consecutive non-~1h gap) further weakens the cluster claim. Honest cycle J diagnosis: 'irregular post-cycle-A; sample of 9 post-A gaps spans 53min to ~5h; insufficient evidence to claim any specific schedule'. Three reframes from cycle I still applicable but each strained by I→J's lack of Founder activity (against event-triggered) and the now-8-missed-fires count required for original-schedule hypothesis.

Heartbeat-duration confound VALIDATED — cycle I's +3s tick (27s vs 24s plateau) was hypothesized as concurrent-Founder-commit confound. Cycle J = natural experiment WITHOUT concurrent Founder activity → duration returned to 24s baseline. Hypothesis confirmed. Pattern now safer to claim: 5-cycle 24s plateau (D+F+G+H+J) with cycle I as documented exception attributable to specific concurrent event.
